Let $A=\lbrace a_{ij} \rbrace$ and $B=\lbrace b_{ij} \rbrace$ be $n \times n$
matrices such that $A^{-1}$ exists.

Define $A(t)=\lbrace a_{ij}(t) \rbrace$ and $B(t)=\lbrace b_{ij}(t) \rbrace$ by

\begin{itemize}

\item $a_{ij}(t)=a_{ij}$ for $i<n$,
\item $a_{nj}(t)=ta_{nj}$,
\item $b_{ij}(t)=b_{ij}$ for $i<n$, and
\item $b_{nj}(t)=tb_{nj}$.

\end{itemize}

For example, if
$A = \begin{bmatrix}
       1 & 2 \\
       3 & 4 \\
     \end{bmatrix}$, then
$A(t) = \begin{bmatrix}
          1  &  2 \\
          3t & 4t \\
        \end{bmatrix}$.

Prove that $A(t)^{-1}B(t)=A^{-1}B$ for $t>0$ and any $n$. (Partial credit will
be given for verifying the result for $n=3$.)
